root.speaker: me
root.annotation: Pre-open: Walk with swag, happy dog face, open exuberant expression. Soft shiny eyes, relaxed facial muscles. The face of a happy dog about to go out and chase tennis balls. Turn the swag on when you spot a target. Manoeuvre your walking path so she walks past you. The street has a 30-second memory. Source: Outlaw Daygame Parts 5-6, Virtua Resi #4, #6

conv1-open.speaker: me

text.en: Excuse me, excuse me... hi.

conv1-open.annotation: Smooth front stop. Dominant but not belligerent. Cheeky smirk, twinkling eyes. Open like you have got a joke that you have not told anyone. Give her space - not too far out like a nervous chode, but not in her face. The matador style: probing, drawing, letting the bull go around. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7

conv1-her1.speaker: her

text.en: Oh... hi?

conv1-her1.annotation: Typical initial response to a smooth front stop. She stops, slightly confused but receptive because of the calm, self-possessed energy. Source: Krauser describes girls stopping and being curious in smooth opens. Outlaw Daygame Part 7

conv1-stack.speaker: me

text.en: I know this is a bit random, but I just saw you walking past and I thought you had a really nice energy about you. Like you have got this kind of Eastern European... are you Russian?

conv1-stack.annotation: Smooth stack. Two observations that compliment, one that teases. Build the tease into the opener itself. Observe three things about her - two complement, one tease. Here the assumption about nationality is a calibrated guess that creates a conversational hook. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 10

conv1-her2.speaker: her

text.en: Ha, no! I am from Czech Republic actually.

conv1-her2.annotation: She corrects the nationality guess, which is investment. She is now engaged in the conversation, justifying herself. This is the hook point - she has asked or contributed something back. Source: Krauser describes nationality guessing as a standard stack technique. Outlaw Daygame Part 10

conv1-vibe1.speaker: me

text.en: Czech! Okay, I can see it now actually. You have got that look... like you spend your weekends in a castle drinking wine and judging people.

conv1-vibe1.annotation: Vibe phase. Going off on a creative ramp - a wild flight of fancy. Krauser describes practicing these ramps as homework: look at something and just spin into creativity with hilarious fantasy scenarios. The tease is playful and paints a vivid picture. Source: Virtua Resi #5 [PuyWa3xwIeU]

conv1-her3.speaker: her

text.en: Ha! That is not true! Well... maybe the wine part.

conv1-her3.annotation: She is laughing and playing along. She starts qualifying and investing. The vibe is working. A flash goes through her eyes - a little crackle of sexual tension. Source: Krauser describes this as the moment where you tick the checkbox of she fancies me. Outlaw Daygame Part 10

conv1-invest1.speaker: me

text.en: I knew it. So what brings you to London then? Don't tell me you are here studying English, that would be very boring.

conv1-invest1.annotation: Investment phase. Toning down the belligerence, being more normal. The challenge is embedded: do not be boring. This is non-combative disagreement - sceptical parroting and challenge that shows boundaries without being rude. Proactively creating the opportunity for her to invest. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 10 [_SrN5T58uu4]

conv1-her4.speaker: her

text.en: No, actually I work here. I am a designer.

conv1-her4.annotation: She is investing properly - giving real information about herself, not just prattling. The purpose of investment is not just to get her talking, she is talking in a direction where you are still leading the conversation. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 10

conv1-close.speaker: me

text.en: A designer, that explains the style. Look, I have got to go meet a friend, but you seem like you would be fun to talk to over a coffee. What is your number?

conv1-close.annotation: Close. Time constraint mentioned naturally. Direct number ask. Not asking for permission to take her number - telling her you want it. Source: Standard London Daygame Model close. Outlaw Daygame Part 7

conv1-her5.speaker: her

text.en: Okay, sure. It is 0-7-7...

conv1-her5.annotation: She gives the number. In a smooth set the compliance ladder has been built gradually through the matador style. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7

conv2-open.speaker: me

text.en: Hey! You! You! Yeah, girl in the black dress! Miserable face! You, stop!

conv2-open.annotation: Belligerent shout-stop from a distance. Krauser describes doing a lot of this in Kiev: a girl walks past, he shouts from distance to stop her. Hands pointing, finger wagging. This requires high vibe - cannot do it as a first set of the day, it will be incongruent. Smashing into them like a battering ram. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7 [rRcPvDj7x90]

conv2-her1.speaker: her

text.en: What?! Me??

conv2-her1.annotation: Brain fry is starting. She is confused but stopped. The belligerent energy is overwhelmingly masculine - keying into her submission fantasies. Like an EMP on a robot - she spazzes out within seconds. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 9 [IZB-_mImb5o]

conv2-stack.speaker: me

text.en: Yeah you. Don't give me that look. I just saw you walking past all moody and I thought, she looks like trouble. What are you, some kind of off-duty spy?

conv2-stack.annotation: Belligerent stack. Hands on both her shoulders - different type of kino to smooth. The tease is built in: calling her moody is the boundary-draw that shows you are not an ass-kisser. The spy assumption is a creative ramp. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7, Part 10

conv2-her2.speaker: her

text.en: Ha ha, oh my god! I... no! I am not a spy!

conv2-her2.annotation: Brain fry in action. She cannot finish sentences properly, she is giggly and squirrely. Like the Serbian girl Krauser describes: she would try to talk and just start laughing. It is a complete disconnect between logic and hindbrain. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 9 [IZB-_mImb5o]

conv2-vibe1.speaker: me

text.en: That is exactly what a spy would say. I bet you have got like three passports and a secret apartment full of maps and disguises.

conv2-vibe1.annotation: Creative ramp - spinning into fantasy. Wild flight of fancy. Krauser describes this as homework: look at something and just go off on these wild flights of fancy where you are spinning into creativity. The belligerence is toned down now - she fancies you, so attraction is done, you do not have to keep flogging the dead horse. Source: Virtua Resi #5 [PuyWa3xwIeU], Outlaw Daygame Part 10

conv2-her3.speaker: her

text.en: You are crazy! Ha ha, okay, only two passports.

conv2-her3.annotation: She is playing along with the frame, qualifying herself within the fantasy. Buzzing energy from the belligerent opener has settled into playful vibe. Source: Krauser describes girls in high-spike sets as fizzy and bubbly. Outlaw Daygame Part 9

conv2-invest1.speaker: me

text.en: I knew it. Alright spy girl, before you disappear into the shadows, what are you actually doing in this part of town?

conv2-invest1.annotation: Toning it down, making it comfortable. After ticking the checkbox that she fancies you, dial the energy down. This is like hook point - she wants to talk, so you can relax. Still keeping the playful frame from the vibe phase as a thread. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 10 [_SrN5T58uu4]

conv2-her4.speaker: her

text.en: I was just shopping. I just moved here last month from Ukraine.

conv2-her4.annotation: Real investment. She is sharing genuine information, not just fizzing. This is proper investment where the conversation has substance. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 10 describes the difference between prattle and real investment

conv2-close.speaker: me

text.en: Ukraine, nice. Look, I am about to head off but you seem fun. Give me your number and we will grab a coffee this week.

conv2-close.annotation: Direct close. Not asking permission - telling her the plan. The belligerent frame carries through to the close: it is assumed, not negotiated. Source: Standard London Daygame Model close

conv2-her5.speaker: her

text.en: Ha ha, okay. You are so funny. Here...

conv2-her5.annotation: She is still giggling from the brain fry but compliant. The submission fantasy from the belligerent energy has made compliance feel natural. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 9

conv3-open.speaker: me

text.en: Oh no, no, no, no. You. Girl. You cannot look at me like that.

conv3-open.annotation: Accusational opener. She gave an IOI (or you pretend she did) and you call her out on it. Belligerent style - you are on the offensive. Creating the opportunity rather than waiting for it. This requires spotting the sideways glance, the double glance, or the held stare that Krauser describes as IOIs. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7 [rRcPvDj7x90], Part 6 [kL3eu6-8xI0]

conv3-her1.speaker: her

text.en: What? Look at you like what?!

conv3-her1.annotation: She is immediately on the back foot, flustered. The accusation creates instant engagement because she has to defend herself. Delightfully intimidating - a different type of masculine presence to bathe in. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7

conv3-stack.speaker: me

text.en: Like that. With those eyes. You are checking me out in the middle of the street. I do not know what kind of men you are used to but where I am from, we at least introduce ourselves first.

conv3-stack.annotation: Doubling down on the accusation playfully. This is the battering ram style - pushing your frame onto the girl. It is power-based, focused on dominance. She enjoys being overwhelmed, she enjoys the submission. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7, Part 2 [cLFkcZMqRa0]

conv3-her2.speaker: her

text.en: I was NOT checking you out! Oh my god...

conv3-her2.annotation: Protesting but smiling. She is investing by defending herself. The more she protests, the more she is engaged. This is brain fry territory - you are crashing her computer and hacking directly into the BIOS. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 9

conv3-vibe1.speaker: me

text.en: Okay, okay. I believe you. Thousands would not, but I believe you. So where are you from? You have got this interesting look, like you could be from anywhere.

conv3-vibe1.annotation: Toning it down after the spike. Switching from belligerence to smoothness. The tease is over, now showing the panda bear underneath the leather jacket - the soft dominance wrapped in the hard exterior. Krauser describes himself as a panda bear in a leather jacket. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 2 [cLFkcZMqRa0], Part 10

conv3-her3.speaker: her

text.en: Ha, I am from Serbia actually.

conv3-her3.annotation: She has relaxed. The switch from belligerence to warmth is the fractionation effect - hot to cold, on to off. She is now investing naturally. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 2

conv3-invest1.speaker: me

text.en: Serbia! I love Belgrade. Beautiful city, crazy nightlife. What do you do here, let me guess... you are either a model or a lawyer. There is no in-between with Serbian women.

conv3-invest1.annotation: Investment phase. Showing knowledge and personality. The guess is a compliment wrapped in a tease. Creating the opportunity for her to talk about herself in a direction you are leading. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 10

conv3-her4.speaker: her

text.en: Ha ha! Neither actually. I am studying architecture.

conv3-her4.annotation: Proper investment. She is talking in a direction he is leading. Not prattling aimlessly. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 10

conv3-close.speaker: me

text.en: Architecture, even better. Look, I need to run, but I want to hear more about this. Give me your number.

conv3-close.annotation: Close. Direct, assumptive. The frame from the accusational opener is maintained - you are the one deciding what happens. Source: Standard London Daygame Model close

conv3-her5.speaker: her

text.en: Okay, sure! It was nice talking to you.

conv3-her5.annotation: Compliant close. The set went from belligerent opener through brain fry to comfortable investment - the full arc from hard dominance to soft dominance. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 2

conv4-open.speaker: me

text.en: Stop, stop, stop, stop, stop, stop.

conv4-open.annotation: Belligerent front stop. Pure belligerence - the opener is just the command to stop, repeated. No excuse me, no compliment, just raw dominance. Krauser says: that is my opener. It is different, it is belligerent. Only works when in high vibe - if you try this as your first set of the day, it will be super incongruent and she will tell you to fuck off. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7 [rRcPvDj7x90]

conv4-her1.speaker: her

text.en: Oh! What... what is happening?

conv4-her1.annotation: Pure brain fry. She is overheated, giggly, like one of the teenage girls at a Beatles concert. Like the EMP effect Krauser saw when Steve Jabba walked into the restaurant and the waitress completely spazzed out within two seconds. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 9 [IZB-_mImb5o]

conv4-stack.speaker: me

text.en: Relax, relax. I just had to stop you. You look like a little marshmallow walking through the snow in that hat. Where did you get that thing?

conv4-stack.annotation: Belligerent kino - touching her hat. Krauser describes: oh look at this hat, it is like a marshmallow fell on your head, and then you play with the hat. Grabbing accessories early and fast with childish exuberance - like you have the right to touch her. Bull in a china shop. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 8 [E8LdbTsJbTY]

conv4-her2.speaker: her

text.en: Ha ha! It is from Zara! A marshmallow, really??

conv4-her2.annotation: She is laughing and defending her fashion choice - investment. She could walk away but she is staying and engaging. Hook point achieved. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 8

conv4-vibe1.speaker: me

text.en: Zara marshmallow collection, I love it. You know what, you remind me of one of those little animals... like a fox. Sneaky but cute.

conv4-vibe1.annotation: Comparing girls to animals - Krauser describes this as a common technique that is now autopilot for him. First time he did it, it was a big deal. Now it is the three thousandth time. The nonchalance is what makes it work. Source: Virtua Resi #2 [8xw8sEUI0Pg]

conv4-her3.speaker: her

text.en: A fox! I do not know if that is a compliment or not!

conv4-her3.annotation: She is unsure if it is a compliment - that ambiguity is the point. It is a tease that requires her to process it. The awkward pause, the little flash through her eyes - sexual tension is building. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 10

conv4-invest1.speaker: me

text.en: It is definitely a compliment. Foxes are smart and beautiful. So what is your story, what are you doing with your Wednesday afternoon?

conv4-invest1.annotation: Toning down. Giving the compliment straight after the tease - the soft dominance emerging from behind the hard. Like the castle on the hill: first she checked the walls and the moat (hard dominance in the belligerent opener), now she is checking the sofas and the tapestries (soft dominance in the investment phase). Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 2 [cLFkcZMqRa0]

conv4-her4.speaker: her

text.en: I just finished my shift at the gallery. I work in art, contemporary stuff.

conv4-her4.annotation: Real investment. She is sharing details about her life and career. Not just prattle but substance. The conversation has natural flow. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 10

conv4-close.speaker: me

text.en: Contemporary art? Now I am definitely interested. Look, I need to head off, but let us continue this. What is your number?

conv4-close.annotation: Close. The interest is genuine and calibrated to what she just said. Time constraint, then the direct ask. Source: London Daygame Model standard close

conv4-her5.speaker: her

text.en: Sure! Do you actually like art or are you just saying that?

conv4-her5.annotation: She is investing even at the close - testing if the interest is real. This is a good sign. She wants the number exchange to lead somewhere. Source: Krauser describes girls who invest at the close as higher quality leads

conv4-final.speaker: me

text.en: I genuinely do. But I will prove that over coffee. Give me your number.

conv4-final.annotation: Handling the close-test with a mini bounce to a future date. Maintaining frame - you do not justify, you redirect to the date. Source: Standard London Daygame Model

conv5-open.speaker: me

text.en: You, you, you! Stop! Three girls, three girls! You! Stop!

conv5-open.annotation: Belligerent group stop. Krauser describes opening a three-set in Kiev after one of the girls gave an IOI: you, you, stop, you, three girls, three girls, you, stop. Crazy one, you, you, you. Then: sorry girls, sorry, I do not mean to interrupt. It is belligerent but then immediately calibrated. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7 [rRcPvDj7x90]

conv5-her1.speaker: her

text.en: Oh my god, what?? Us??

conv5-her1.annotation: The group is startled but stopped. In a group open, you address the group but your eye contact and body language makes clear which girl you want. The IOI girl knows it is for her. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7

conv5-stack.speaker: me

text.en: Sorry girls, sorry, I do not mean to interrupt. But your friend here, she just gave me this look and I had to come over. I could not just let that go.

conv5-stack.annotation: Krauser's exact calibration after a belligerent group stop: sorry girls, sorry, I do not mean to interrupt. Addresses the group politely but then singles out the target. The accusation that she gave you a look is the hook. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7 [rRcPvDj7x90]

conv5-her2.speaker: her

text.en: I did not give you a look! She is lying!

conv5-her2.annotation: She protests to her friends but is smiling. Her friends are giggling. The social proof of being bold enough to stop three girls adds to the attraction. She is defending herself - investment. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7

conv5-vibe1.speaker: me

text.en: See? She is blushing. You two are my witnesses. Your friend is clearly a dangerous woman. What is she like normally, is she always this forward?

conv5-vibe1.annotation: Engaging the friends as allies. Creating a fun group dynamic where everyone is laughing. The friends become invested in the interaction. Krauser describes this as using the group energy - the friends validate the interaction. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7

conv5-her3.speaker: her

text.en: Oh my god, shut up! My friends are going to kill me. Yes, okay, MAYBE I looked.

conv5-her3.annotation: She admits to the IOI in front of her friends. Major investment. Her friends are egging her on. The social dynamic is working in your favor. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7

conv5-invest1.speaker: me

text.en: I knew it. Alright girls, I am going to steal your friend for thirty seconds. What is your name?

conv5-invest1.annotation: Mini bounce - physically separating her from the group by even just a step. Taking control of her body, which is what the whole process of seduction is about. Asking permission from the group while physically leading the target. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 11 [v31iSEwaGFI]

conv5-her4.speaker: her

text.en: It is Katya. And you are...?

conv5-her4.annotation: She asks his name back - major hook point indicator. She is now invested in the one-on-one. Her friends are watching but not intervening. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 10 describes hook point as when the girl asks you a question

conv5-close.speaker: me

text.en: Nick. Look Katya, your friends are waiting and I need to go, but I want to see you again. Give me your number and I will message you.

conv5-close.annotation: Close with social awareness of the group dynamic. Acknowledging the friends waiting shows calibration. Direct number ask. Source: Standard London Daygame Model close

conv5-her5.speaker: her

text.en: Okay! My friends are going to tease me about this all day.

conv5-her5.annotation: She gives the number while acknowledging the social impact. The friends teasing her is actually good - it anchors the memory of the interaction and makes her more likely to reply to texts. Source: Krauser describes group dynamics in Kiev opens

conv6-open.speaker: me

text.en: Excuse me, excuse me. I know this is random but I just noticed something about you.

conv6-open.annotation: Smooth front stop with observation tease. The classic London Daygame Model opener with the two-compliment-one-tease observation structure built in. Self-possessed, solid frame, social acuity. Like the matador probing and drawing. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7 [rRcPvDj7x90], Part 10 [_SrN5T58uu4]

conv6-her1.speaker: her

text.en: Oh? What did you notice?

conv6-her1.annotation: Curious response. The smooth open creates intrigue rather than shock. She wants to know what you saw. This is the slow cooker effect - unlike the brain fry of belligerence, smoothness warms her gradually. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 2 [cLFkcZMqRa0]

conv6-stack.speaker: me

text.en: Well, you have got this really elegant walk, beautiful style... but then I saw your shoes and I thought, what is going on here? That is a bit weird, is it not?

conv6-stack.annotation: The observation stack: two compliments (elegant walk, beautiful style), one tease (the shoes). Krauser describes: you look at her and you pick something she is wearing or carrying. What is going on here? That is a bit fucking weird, is it not? You have just created that opportunity for a boundary. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 10 [_SrN5T58uu4]

conv6-her2.speaker: her

text.en: My shoes?! What is wrong with my shoes?!

conv6-her2.annotation: She defends herself - investment through justification. Krauser describes this as the girl justifying herself after a tease, which shows the boundary and masculine polarity. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 10

conv6-vibe1.speaker: me

text.en: No no, I am joking, they are cool. They just surprised me. You look very put together but then the shoes are all colourful and wild. It is like you have got a secret rebel side. Like a librarian who goes to raves on weekends.

conv6-vibe1.annotation: The smooth tease resolved into a compliment. This is the probing boxer style - jab and feint to get her to create the opening, then take it. The creative ramp builds a personality picture. Showing social acuity by reading her style as a personality signal. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 8 [E8LdbTsJbTY], Virtua Resi #5

conv6-her3.speaker: her

text.en: Ha ha! That is... actually kind of accurate. I am an accountant but I love music festivals.

conv6-her3.annotation: She is qualifying herself, confirming the read. This is the payoff of the smooth style - she enjoys the subtle interplay of the dance and your mastery of the steps. The social acuity creates attraction through being seen and understood. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 7

conv6-invest1.speaker: me

text.en: Really? Which festivals? And please do not say Glastonbury, everyone says Glastonbury.

conv6-invest1.annotation: Non-combative challenge in the investment phase. The sceptical parroting Krauser describes: really? Why? It shows boundaries without being rude - non-combative but still a challenge. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 10 [_SrN5T58uu4]

conv6-her4.speaker: her

text.en: No, actually more like Sonar in Barcelona, or Exit in Serbia. Have you been?

conv6-her4.annotation: Deep investment. She is sharing real interests and asking if you have shared experiences. She is leading the conversation towards common ground - a very good sign. Source: Krauser describes good investment as her talking in a direction where you are still leading

conv6-close.speaker: me

text.en: Exit, yes! Belgrade is amazing. Look, I would love to talk more about this but I actually need to go. Let us swap numbers and carry on this conversation.

conv6-close.annotation: Close that flows naturally from the conversation. The shared interest in travel and music creates a natural bridge to the number exchange. Time constraint, direct ask. Source: London Daygame Model standard close

conv6-her5.speaker: her

text.en: Yeah, definitely! Here, I will give you mine.

conv6-her5.annotation: She proactively offers the number. In a smooth set that has flowed well, the number exchange feels like the natural next step rather than an ask. Source: Krauser describes smooth sets as slow cookers that build steadily

conv7-open.speaker: me

text.en: Hey. I just saw you walk past and... honestly, you are far too glamorous for this street. What is happening?

conv7-open.annotation: Gutter game opener - the evening variant. High covert sexuality, getting in close, holding the hand a long time, putting the eyes on. Filtering for vulnerability and availability: she is alone at night, walking slowly, off from the herd. The subconscious decision to make herself vulnerable. Source: Virtua Resi #1 [LCMW0fFEKQk]

conv7-her1.speaker: her

text.en: Ha, thank you! I just finished dinner with a friend actually.

conv7-her1.annotation: Gutter game filter check: she is alone, she has finished her plans, she is available. These are the vulnerability signals Krauser scans for: just finished whatever she was doing, off by herself, nothing better to do. Source: Virtua Resi #1 [LCMW0fFEKQk]

conv7-stack.speaker: me

text.en: Ah so your friend abandoned you and left you wandering the streets alone at night. That is very irresponsible of them. You look like you could use a bodyguard.

conv7-stack.annotation: Gutter game stack - covertly sexual and protective framing. Playing on the vulnerability in a fun way. The bodyguard frame establishes a man-woman dynamic immediately. In gutter game you go in highly covertly sexual. Source: Virtua Resi #1 [LCMW0fFEKQk]

conv7-her2.speaker: her

text.en: Ha! Maybe I do. It is getting dark and I do not know this area very well.

conv7-her2.annotation: Confirming the vulnerability signals: she does not know the area, it is getting dark, she is open to company. In gutter game the eye dates become open-ended because it is already night and alcohol is normal. Source: Virtua Resi #1

conv7-vibe1.speaker: me

text.en: Well you are in luck because I happen to be an excellent tour guide. Terrible sense of direction, but very entertaining. Where are you from? You do not look like a local.

conv7-vibe1.annotation: Smooth vibing with self-deprecation. Setting up the instant date proposition. The self-deprecating humor (terrible sense of direction) is the soft dominance - making the castle a nice place to be, not just a safe one. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 2 [cLFkcZMqRa0], Virtua Resi #1

conv7-her3.speaker: her

text.en: I am from Poland, just visiting for a few days. I am a tourist!

conv7-her3.annotation: Gutter game gold: she is a tourist. Krauser describes the tourist as the ideal gutter game target: tourists in a rucksack, 8pm, been out all day, finished what she was doing, vulnerable by being a tourist, off by herself, nothing better to do. Highly likely to be whisked up. Source: Virtua Resi #1 [LCMW0fFEKQk]

conv7-invest1.speaker: me

text.en: A Polish tourist! Perfect. Well look, I was about to grab a drink nearby. It would be criminal to let you wander around lost. Come on, there is a nice bar just there.

conv7-invest1.annotation: The gutter game instant date proposition. In gutter game you are trying to get it done that night - the eye date is open-ended, already starting at night, alcohol is normal. The proposition is framed as the natural, obvious next step. Mini bounce: come on, let us walk over there. Source: Virtua Resi #1 [LCMW0fFEKQk], Outlaw Daygame Part 11

conv7-her4.speaker: her

text.en: I... okay, sure, why not! I was not doing anything else tonight.

conv7-her4.annotation: The gutter game close: she comes with you. I was not doing anything else tonight confirms the vulnerability and availability. The instant date leads to drinks, which leads to escalation. This is why gutter game has a much higher same-night conversion rate. Source: Virtua Resi #1

conv7-close.speaker: me

text.en: Brilliant. Come on then, it is just round the corner. So tell me, what has been your favourite thing about the city so far?

conv7-close.annotation: Walking her to the venue. Soft kino starts: hand on the small of her back guiding her, or holding her hand as they walk. The Russian arm drag - taking the wrist and pulling her around. The physical escalation begins subtly as you walk together. Source: Outlaw Daygame Part 11 [v31iSEwaGFI], Virtua Resi #1
